message:
fix(codegen): Add __bool__() wrapper for class comparison conditions

When a class instance with comparison dunder methods (like __gt__) is used
in an if condition, the comparison result may be a non-bool type (e.g.,
SymbolicBool). This caused Zig compilation errors because the if statement
expects a bool.

The fix detects class comparison conditions and wraps them with .__bool__()
to properly convert the result to bool:
- Inside try body: try (try comparison).__bool__()
- Outside try: ((comparison) catch unreachable).__bool__() catch false

This fixes test_bool compilation which was failing with:
"error: expected type 'bool', found '!*SymbolicBool'"
